description:
Offered: every semester
This course covers central principles and practices of journalism. Students will study ideas of credibility, accuracy, the First Amendment, the roles and responsibilites of the press, and the function of the journalist within a democratic society. Students will discuss the challenges facing the industry, the impact of changing priorities, and consumption of news products. This course will provide a foundation for students interested in professional news writing. Cross-listed with COMM 2800
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200 or HON 1200
Note: Credit is not given for both ENGL 2800 and COMM 2800 .
Meets General Education Requirement(s): CV, DP
